query: what's that trick where you take stuff outside the loop so it doesn't repeat calculations
answer:
Loop optimization techniques include:

1. Code Motion 
   - Definition: Move computations outside the loop if they produce the same result every iteration.
   - Benefit: 
     • Avoids repeated computation
     • Improves loop efficiency

2. Induction Variable Elimination 
   - Definition: Simplifies variables that change predictably in a loop
   - Explanation: 
     • Eliminates multiplication inside loop
     • Uses addition instead (faster)
